successCriteria.description: src/modules/health/health.model.ts exists and exports ProcessUptime interface with startTime, currentTime, uptimeSeconds properties and calculateUptime function
src/modules/health/health.service.ts exists and exports HealthService interface and HealthServiceImpl class
HealthServiceImpl implements HealthService interface with getUptime method that returns a Promise<ProcessUptime>
HealthServiceImpl correctly calculates uptime in seconds using Date difference from process start time
All TypeScript interfaces compile without errors using the project's TypeScript configuration
ProcessUptime entity is a transient domain concept (not persisted) - no SQL table needed
The new ProcessUptime domain entity is added to ARCHITECTURE.md documentation in the health module section

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing.
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled.
outOfScope: Implementation of UptimeAuditRepository for audit logging
Implementation of health controller with RBAC and validation
Creation of Fastify route for GET /uptime endpoint
Integration of audit logging into uptime endpoint
Any database persistence for ProcessUptime entity
Any changes to existing modules (employee, policy, balance, leave, notification)
Any changes to shared types beyond potential imports

ambiguities.description: Should ProcessUptime interface have a getUptime() method (as in first architecture notes) or should calculation be done via separate calculateUptime function (as in detailed architecture)?
Should HealthService.getUptime() return Promise<number> (as in first notes) or Promise<ProcessUptime> (as in detailed architecture)?
Should the calculateUptime function be synchronous or asynchronous?
